title: NERC CIP Electronic Access Point Monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-generated from legacy markdown 'Regulations' line during the per-UC migration. Requires SME review to fill in version, clause, mode, and assurance for NERC CIP.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP unknown is enforced — Splunk UC-10.14.16: NERC CIP Electronic Access Point Monitoring.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-10.14.16' running on index=ot_security perimeter firewalls, VPN, jump hosts tagged bes_asset=1, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=ot_security` perimeter firewalls, VPN, jump hosts tagged `bes_asset=1`
spl:
index=ot_security sourcetype="ics:firewall" bes_asset=1
| lookup cip_electronic_access_points dest OUTPUT eap_name esp_id
| where isnotnull(eap_name)
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last by eap_name, esp_id, src, user, action
| eval cip_standard="CIP-005"
| table cip_standard, eap_name, esp_id, src, user, action, count, first, last
description: Supports NERC CIP-005 by monitoring access to BES Cyber Systems at controlled Electronic Security Perimeter entry points for audit readiness and breach detection.
value: Supports NERC CIP-005 by monitoring access to BES Cyber Systems at controlled Electronic Security Perimeter entry points for audit readiness and breach detection.
implementation: Map Splunk ES and OT Security Add-on NERC CIP dashboards to registered EAPs. Retain evidence per regional entity policy. Correlate with access authorization lists per CIP-005.
visualization: ESP topology summary, Timeline of EAP sessions, Compliance summary by site.
knownFalsePositives: Compliance dashboards can go yellow during documentation lag, retroactive CIP baselines, or tool upgrades — verify the underlying control, not just the reporting day.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_security` perimeter firewalls, VPN, jump hosts tagged `bes_asset=1`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map Splunk ES and OT Security Add-on NERC CIP dashboards to registered EAPs. Retain evidence per regional entity policy. Correlate with access authorization lists per CIP-005.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_security sourcetype="ics:firewall" bes_asset=1
| lookup cip_electronic_access_points dest OUTPUT eap_name esp_id
| where isnotnull(eap_name)
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last by eap_name, esp_id, src, user, action
| eval cip_standard="CIP-005"
| table cip_standard, eap_name, esp_id, src, user, action, count, first, last
```

Understanding this SPL

**NERC CIP Electronic Access Point Monitoring** — Supports NERC CIP-005 by monitoring access to BES Cyber Systems at controlled Electronic Security Perimeter entry points for audit readiness and breach detection.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_security` perimeter firewalls, VPN, jump hosts tagged `bes_asset=1`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OT Security Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_security; **sourcetype**: ics:firewall. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_security, sourcetype="ics:firewall". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(eap_name)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by eap_name, esp_id, src, user, action** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cip_standard** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **NERC CIP Electronic Access Point Monitoring**): table cip_standard, eap_name, esp_id, src, user, action, count, first, last

Step 3 — Validate
Compare failed or disabled searches in Splunk to the **OT Security Add-on** configuration UI and the **Saved Searches** list so names and schedules line up. Spot-check one failure against `scheduler.log` around the run time.



Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: ESP topology summary, Timeline of EAP sessions, Compliance summary by site.
grandmaExplanation: We help prove we only let remote access the way the grid security standards say we should, in one place to audit.
